findings.description: complete() does not clear reason_code — a stuck-reclaimed job that later succeeds retains reason_code='timeout' on a completed job, misleading operators. The reclaim RPC writes reason_code='timeout' on retry-eligible jobs. If the retried job subsequently succeeds, queue.complete() only sets status and completed_at, leaving the stale reason_code. Fix: add reason_code=None to the complete() update payload.
findings.resolution: Added reason_code=None to queue.complete() update payload.

findings.description: reclaim_stuck_jobs RPC does not set failed_at when moving exhausted jobs to failed status, unlike the Python queue.fail() path (queue.py:148) which writes failed_at. The plan doc (Task 2, line 109) also specifies failed_at should be set. Add: failed_at = CASE WHEN s.attempts >= s.max_attempts THEN now() ELSE failed_at END to the UPDATE SET clause.
findings.resolution: Added failed_at = CASE WHEN s.attempts >= s.max_attempts THEN now() ELSE failed_at END to UPDATE SET clause. Migration was branch-only (commit 87a08baa), so existing file was edited rather than creating a new migration.

findings.description: Unrelated migration (DEV-297 user preferences) included in this branch. This file is tagged as DEV-297 cold-start preference onboarding and has no relation to DEV-312 reason codes. Should be removed from this branch to avoid shipping unrelated schema changes.
findings.resolution: Removed via git rm. File was introduced in the same branch-only commit (87a08baa) as the DEV-312 migrations.

findings.description: All scheduler exception types (CancelledError, generic Exception) map to PROVIDER_ERROR — no differentiation for bad_input or dependency_failed. The plan doc acknowledges this as intentional V1 behavior.
findings.resolution: No code change. Intentional V1 behavior explicitly acknowledged in plan doc. Differentiated reason codes for CancelledError vs generic Exception deferred to post-V1 observability work.

findings.description: Design doc says retry_exhausted maps to terminal status dead_letter, but queue.fail() uses JobStatus.FAILED (not DEAD_LETTER). The backfill migration also targets status='failed'. Doc should be corrected to match implementation.
findings.resolution: Updated design doc: retry_exhausted maps to 'failed' (not 'dead_letter'), timeout maps to 'pending/failed' (not 'pending/dead_letter'), Scope section updated, backfill section updated.
